name: Windows-MCP
description: MCP Server that enables Claude to interact with Windows OS
long_description:
Windows-MCP is an open-source project that enables seamless integration between AI agents and the Windows operating system. Acting as an MCP server, it bridges the gap between large language models (LLMs) and the Windows OS, allowing agents to perform tasks such as **file navigation, application control, UI interaction, QA testing, and more**.

**KEY FEATURES**
- **Seamless Windows Integration**: Interacts natively with Windows UI elements, opens applications, controls windows, simulates user input, and more.
- **Use Any LLM (Vision Optional)**: Does not rely on traditional computer vision techniques or fine-tuned models. Works with any LLM, reducing complexity and setup time.
- **Rich Toolset for UI Automation**: Includes tools for keyboard and mouse control, window management, and capturing window or UI state.
- **Lightweight & Open-Source**: Minimal dependencies with full source code available under the MIT license.
- **Customizable & Extendable**: Easily adapt or extend tools to suit custom automation workflows or AI integrations.
- **Real-Time Interaction**: Typical latency between actions ranges from `0.2` to `0.9` seconds, depending on system load, active applications, and LLM inference speed.

**MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS**
- **Python 3.13+**

anonymized_telemetry.title: Anonymized Telemetry
anonymized_telemetry.description: Collects basic usage data to help improve the MCP server. No personal information, tool arguments, or tool outputs are tracked.

profile_snapshot.title: Profile Snapshot Performance
profile_snapshot.description: Enable detailed performance logging for desktop snapshots and screenshots. Useful for troubleshooting latency.

screenshot_backend.title: Screenshot Backend
screenshot_backend.description: The engine used to capture screenshots. 'auto' selects the fastest available backend (DXGI -> MSS -> Pillow). Use 'pillow' if you encounter capture issues.

debug.title: Debug Mode
debug.description: Enable debug mode for verbose logging and troubleshooting.

tools.name: App
tools.description: Manages Windows applications: 'launch' opens an application, 'resize' adjusts size/position of a window, 'switch' brings a window into focus.

tools.name: PowerShell
tools.description: Executes PowerShell commands for file system navigation, process management, network operations, and administrative tasks.

tools.name: FileSystem
tools.description: File system operations: 'read', 'write', 'copy', 'move', 'delete', 'list', 'search', 'info'. Relative paths resolve from the Desktop folder.

tools.name: Screenshot
tools.description: Captures a fast screenshot with cursor position and active window info. Use display=[0] or display=[0,1] to target specific screens.

tools.name: Snapshot
tools.description: Captures full desktop state including interactive elements with coordinates. Set use_vision=True to include a screenshot.

tools.name: Click
tools.description: Performs mouse clicks at coordinates [x,y] or by UI element label/id. Supports left/right/middle buttons and single/double-click.

tools.name: Type
tools.description: Types text at coordinates [x,y] or by UI element label/id. Supports clearing existing text and pressing Enter after input.

tools.name: Scroll
tools.description: Scrolls at coordinates [x,y] or by UI element label/id. Supports vertical and horizontal scrolling.

tools.name: Move
tools.description: Moves the mouse cursor to coordinates [x,y] or by UI element label/id. Supports drag-and-drop with drag=True.

tools.name: Wait
tools.description: Pauses execution for a specified duration in seconds.

tools.name: Scrape
tools.description: Fetches content from a URL or the active browser tab.

tools.name: MultiSelect
tools.description: Selects multiple UI items by coordinates or labels. Use press_ctrl=True for multi-selection.

tools.name: MultiEdit
tools.description: Enters text into multiple input fields at once using coordinates or labels.

tools.name: Clipboard
tools.description: Manages the Windows clipboard. mode='get' reads content, mode='set' writes text.

tools.name: Process
tools.description: Lists and kills system processes. mode='list' for process info, mode='kill' to terminate by PID or name.

tools.name: Notification
tools.description: Sends a Windows toast notification with a title and message.

tools.name: Registry
tools.description: Accesses the Windows Registry. Supports get, set, delete, and list operations.
